title: Hydraulic Engineering
outline: This course aims to develop an understanding of flow in pipelines: laminar and turbulent flow – Reynolds; head losses in pipelines and fittings; the design of pipe systems. Pump selection. Open channel flow: the steady flow equations; Froude; uniform, gradually and rapidly varied flow; hydraulic structures, e.g. flumes, weirs, spillways and control gates.
lecture_times: See departmental timetable and official class schedule.
dp_requirements: Please refer to the official course handout document for detailed information regarding the DP requirements for this course.
